name: eslint
context: Lint and auto-fix JavaScript/TypeScript code with ESLint — configure rules, plugins, and flat config
info: ESLint is the standard static analysis tool for JavaScript and TypeScript projects. It finds and optionally auto-fixes code quality issues, enforces style conventions, and catches bugs before they ship. ESLint 9+ uses flat config (eslint.config.js/mjs/cjs) as the default configuration format, replacing the legacy .eslintrc format. Agents interact with ESLint to diagnose lint errors, apply --fix for auto-correction, configure rules per-project, integrate plugins like typescript-eslint and eslint-plugin-react, and migrate legacy configs to flat config. The CLI surfaces in CI pipelines, pre-commit hooks, and editor integrations. This ecosystem covers configuration ([[eslint/config]]), rule management ([[eslint/rules]]), auto-fixing ([[eslint/fix]]), the flat config system ([[eslint/flat-config]]), plugin integration ([[eslint/plugins]]), and common troubleshooting patterns ([[eslint/troubleshooting]]).
triggers: how do I disable an eslint rule
body:
# ESLint

ESLint statically analyzes JavaScript and TypeScript to find problems. It is fully configurable: every rule can be turned on or off, and many rules have additional options.

## Installation

```bash
# npm
npm init @eslint/config@latest

# pnpm
pnpm create @eslint/config@latest

# yarn
yarn create @eslint/config
```

Or install manually:

```bash
npm install --save-dev eslint @eslint/js
```

## Quick Start

Create `eslint.config.mjs`:

```javascript
import js from "@eslint/js";

export default [
  js.configs.recommended,
  {
    rules: {
      "no-unused-vars": "warn",
      "no-console": "off",
    },
  },
];
```

Run:

```bash
npx eslint .           # Lint everything
npx eslint --fix .     # Lint and auto-fix
npx eslint src/        # Lint specific directory
```

## CLI Reference

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|----------|
| `npx eslint .` | Lint current directory |
| `npx eslint --fix .` | Auto-fix fixable issues |
| `npx eslint --fix-dry-run .` | Preview fixes without writing |
| `npx eslint --cache .` | Cache results for faster reruns |
| `npx eslint --max-warnings 0 .` | Fail on any warnings (CI) |
| `npx eslint --debug .` | Debug output for troubleshooting |
| `npx eslint --print-config src/app.js` | Show resolved config for a file |
| `npx eslint --inspect-config` | Launch interactive config inspector |

## Exit Codes

| Code | Meaning |
|------|----------|
| 0 | No errors |
| 1 | Lint errors found |
| 2 | Configuration or internal error |

## Ecosystem

- **[[eslint/config]]** — Initialize and configure ESLint for a project
- **[[eslint/rules]]** — Enable, disable, and configure individual lint rules
- **[[eslint/fix]]** — Auto-fix lint errors with `--fix` and programmatic API
- **[[eslint/flat-config]]** — Understand the flat config format (eslint.config.js)
- **[[eslint/plugins]]** — Integrate plugins like typescript-eslint, react, import
- **[[eslint/troubleshooting]]** — Debug common ESLint issues and errors

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `ESLINT_USE_FLAT_CONFIG` | Set to `false` to force legacy config (deprecated) |
| `ESLINT_D_PPID` | Used by eslint_d for daemon mode |
| `DEBUG` | Set to `eslint:*` for debug logging |

sections.title: Installation
sections.body: Install ESLint with `npm init @eslint/config@latest` for guided setup, or `npm install --save-dev eslint @eslint/js` for manual installation. The guided setup creates an `eslint.config.mjs` with recommended rules and framework-specific plugins. ESLint 9+ requires Node.js 18.18.0 or later.

sections.title: CLI usage
sections.body: Run `npx eslint .` to lint the current directory. Use `--fix` to auto-fix, `--cache` for performance, `--max-warnings 0` for strict CI, and `--print-config <file>` to debug configuration. Exit code 0 means no errors, 1 means lint errors found, 2 means config/internal error. See [[eslint/fix]] for auto-fix details.

sections.title: Configuration formats
sections.body: ESLint 9+ defaults to flat config (`eslint.config.js`, `.mjs`, `.cjs`). Legacy `.eslintrc.*` files are deprecated. Flat config exports an array of config objects — each can have `files`, `ignores`, `rules`, `plugins`, `languageOptions`, and `linterOptions`. See [[eslint/flat-config]] for the full format and [[eslint/config]] for setup guidance.
